response:
Here's a comprehensive set of unit tests for the `is_valid_email` function using pytest. The tests cover happy paths, edge cases, and error cases:

```python
import pytest
from your_module import is_valid_email  # Replace 'your_module' with the actual module name

class TestIsValidEmail:
    """Test cases for the is_valid_email function."""

    # Happy path tests
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("valid_email", [
        "simple@example.com",
        "very.common@example.com",
        "disposable.style.email.with+symbol@example.com",
        "other.email-with-hyphen@example.com",
        "fully-qualified-domain@example.com",
        "user.name+tag+sorting@example.com",
        "x@example.com",  # one-letter local part
        "example-indeed@strange-example.com",
        "admin@mailserver1",
        "example@s.example",  # see the List of Internet top-level domains
        "\" \"@example.org",  # space between the quotes
        "\"john..doe\"@example.org",  # quoted double dot
        "mailhost!username@example.org",  # bangified host route used for uucp mailers
        "user%example.com@example.org",  # % escaped mail route to user@example.com via example.org
    ])
    def test_valid_emails(self, valid_email):
        """Test that valid email addresses return True."""
        assert is_valid_email(valid_email) is True

    # Edge cases
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("edge_case_email", [
        "a@b.co",  # minimal valid email
        "a" * 64 + "@example.com",  # max local part length (64 chars)
        "a@" + "b" * 255 + ".com",  # max domain length (255 chars)
        "a@b.c",  # shortest possible TLD (2 chars)
        "a@b.中国",  # internationalized domain name (though regex doesn't support this)
    ])
    def test_edge_case_emails(self, edge_case_email):
        """Test edge case email addresses."""
        # Note: The current regex doesn't support internationalized domain names
        if "中国" in edge_case_email:
            assert is_valid_email(edge_case_email) is False
        else:
            assert is_valid_email(edge_case_email) is True

    # Error cases - invalid emails
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("invalid_email", [
        # Missing parts
        "",
        "@example.com",
        "plainaddress",
        "user@.com",
        ".user@example.com",
        "user@.example.com",
        "user@example..com",

        # Invalid characters
        "user@exa mple.com",
        "user@exa\tmple.com",
        "user@exa\nmple.com",
        "user@exa\r\nmple.com",
        "user@exa\"mple.com",
        "user@exa<mple.com",
        "user@exa>mple.com",
        "user@exa[mple.com",
        "user@exa]mple.com",

        # Invalid structure
        "user@example..com",
        "user@example.com.",
        "user@example_com",
        "user@example+com",
        "user@-example.com",
        "user@example-.com",
        "user@example.com-",
        "user@example..com",

        # Too long
        "a" * 65 + "@example.com",  # local part too long
        "a@" + "b" * 256 + ".com",  # domain too long

        # Invalid TLD
        "user@example.c",
        "user@example.123",
        "user@example.abcdefgh",  # TLD too long (though regex allows up to 255)
    ])
    def test_invalid_emails(self, invalid_email):
        """Test that invalid email addresses return False."""
        assert is_valid_email(invalid_email) is False

    # Type error cases
    def test_non_string_input(self):
        """Test that non-string inputs raise TypeError."""
        with pytest.raises(TypeError):
            is_valid_email(None)
        with pytest.raises(TypeError):
            is_valid_email(123)
        with pytest.raises(TypeError):
            is_valid_email([])
        with pytest.raises(TypeError):
            is_valid_email({})
```

### Notes about the test cases:

1. **Happy Path Tests**:
   - Covers various valid email formats according to RFC 5322 (though the regex is simplified)
